parsed_review.summary: CP00-719 is a descriptor-only, no-write, runtime-closed extension of the RP23 External Integrations II finance-integrations P09 Secondary Workflow Slice (M04) and Permission And Audit Binding (M05) over 40 units (RP23.P09.M04.S01-RP23.P09.M05.S20, Risk B). Direct read-only inspection confirms full consistency across baseline, manifest, registry binding/requirements, service/validators/index source, the test suite, the generated contract (latest_pack=CP00-719, validation.valid=true), and the validation script. The pack does not over-claim: manifest production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, and the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification flag, not a runtime or enterprise-trust claim; inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result are correctly in the pending posture awaiting exactly one valid hardened read-only receipt (this one). No-write/no-real-data is comprehensively enforced via a frozen NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and validators.js guards (no finance runtime opening, no permission-decision detail, no audit-event body, no credential/financial payload, synthetic-only); a source scan found no network/fs-write/db/secret usage, only descriptor booleans set false. The authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only and explicitly non-final (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human approval is required for runtime opening, local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and the review request is limited to Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic control-plane evidence (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; the validation script writes only the contract projection artifact, not product state). No prior raw-output/receipt exists for this pack and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON, so there is no malformed/auth-failed evidence to reject. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 provenance note (build/test results are asserted by command-evidence.json, not re-executed under read-only review, but corroborated by direct source inspection). Verdict: PASS — eligible for closeout; final human/enterprise-trust approval remains outside this receipt as expected.

findings.title: Validation/test green results are asserted by command-evidence.json, not re-executed under read-only review
findings.details: The pack's passing results for npm test, npm run validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm run rp23:external-integrations-ii:validate, node --test, git diff --check, etc. (all exit_code 0, recorded 2026-06-15T05:32:19-40Z) are taken from command-evidence.json. As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash) I cannot independently re-execute them. I did corroborate the structural claims by directly inspecting the source: the CP719 binding/requirements in registry.js (lines 426-441, 3739-3833), the descriptor/packet construction in service.js (lines 166-172), the no-write/authority guards in validators.js (line 8), the CP719 tests in test/model.test.js (lines 66-68), and the generated contract (latest_pack=CP00-719 with validation.valid=true and CP719 coverage/descriptor valid:true). The structures are internally consistent and the validators genuinely enforce the stated boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Treat the command-evidence results as claimed (agent-reported) rather than independently re-run within this review; the closeout gate's own Command rerun deliverable plus the H23 Hermes gate provide the authoritative re-execution. No code or evidence change required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; accepted as an inherent property of read-only review. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
